description: Lap 1 rewrites seed/content/a.md to repoint the broken './missing.md' link at the real 'b.md' that already exists in the same directory. The remaining link ('b.md') already resolved, so check_links.py exits 0 on lap 1. Demonstrates the canonical loop reaching DONE with zero API keys and zero external tools.

interactions.agent_output: Ran check_links.py: 1 broken link — content/a.md -> ./missing.md (target does not exist on disk). There is no separate 'advanced guide' page in content/; the sentence was pointing at a page that was never written. Repointing it at the existing introduction page (b.md) instead of leaving a dangling link. Not touching the checker.

actions.content:
# Getting Started with Loop Engineering

Loop Engineering is the discipline of driving an agent against a bounded,
verifiable gate instead of trusting a single unchecked pass.

If you're new here, start with the [introduction](b.md) before going
further. For advanced patterns, see the [introduction](b.md) as well —
it covers the core loop mechanics that later posts build on.

Once you've read the introduction, come back and explore the rest of the
series.

interactions.notes: The fix — check_links.py now exits 0. bl run reaches DONE on lap 1, keyless, no external tool.

interactions.agent_output: Fix already applied; no further changes needed.
interactions.notes: Fallback for any lap beyond 1 — should not be reached in the happy path.
